streams.pump_role: single feed containing all reagents premixed in DES
streams.reasoning: From Chemistry Plan stream_logic: All reagents are stable together at room temperature and must be premixed in DES before entering heated zone. Single feed simplifies pumping of viscous DES and ensures correct stoichiometry. Concentration adjusted from batch 0.5 M to 0.0667 M based on calculation dilution factor.

mixing_order_reasoning: From Chemistry Plan: Single premixed feed eliminates need for mixing. All substrates dissolved in DES at room temperature before entering heated coil reactor. No premature reaction occurs because nitrile oxide generation requires elevated temperature.
chemistry_notes: Thermal 1,3-dipolar cycloaddition proceeding via in-situ nitrile oxide generation from ethyl nitroacetate at 120°C, followed by [3+2] cycloaddition with phenylacetylene. Rate-limiting step is nitrile oxide formation. Flow advantages: (1) Excellent heat transfer in 1 mm ID tubing enables rapid heating to 120°C, (2) Back-pressure regulation allows superheating above solvent bp, (3) Continuous generation and immediate trapping of reactive nitrile oxide minimizes dimerization. Calculation shows Da = 1.97 indicating mixing may limit conversion - but single feed eliminates this concern. BPR required due to gas generation (H2O elimination).

reasoning_per_field.residence_time_min: Analogy 1 used 5 min for photocycloaddition at 200°C. Analogy 3 used 20 min for [3+2] cycloaddition at 60°C. Calculation estimated 15.0 min (range 1.0-22.5 min). Using 15.0 min as it matches calculation center and accounts for lower temperature vs Analogy 1.
reasoning_per_field.flow_rate_mL_min: Calculation: V_R = 7.5 mL, τ = 15 min → Q = 0.5 mL/min. Matches calculation exactly.
reasoning_per_field.temperature_C: Same as batch (120°C) - Chemistry Plan indicates this temperature required for nitrile oxide generation. Analogies show higher temps possible but batch already optimized.
reasoning_per_field.concentration_M: Calculation shows dilution from batch 0.5 M to 0.0667 M due to flow rate requirements. Analogy 1 maintained 0.5 M but different reaction class.
reasoning_per_field.BPR_bar: Calculation: BPR REQUIRED at 5.0 bar due to gas-liquid system (H2O elimination from nitrile oxide formation). Analogy 1 used 34 bar, Analogy 2 used 47.6 bar - using lower 5.0 bar as sufficient for our system.
reasoning_per_field.tubing_material: PFA selected for high temperature operation (120°C) and chemical compatibility with DES. Not photochemistry so transparency not required.
reasoning_per_field.tubing_ID_mm: Calculation specifies 1.0 mm ID for optimal heat transfer and pressure drop (Re = 10.6, laminar flow confirmed).
reasoning_per_field.reactor_volume_mL: Calculation: V_R = 7.5 mL based on τ = 15 min and Q = 0.5 mL/min. Analogy 1 used 30 mL but higher flow rate.
